# Benchmarks

This document records the methodology and the latest numbers for the
stainkit pipeline. Numbers are regenerated automatically by the
benchmark executable:

```bash
./build/bin/stainkit --input data/raw --output data/processed \
                     --benchmark --csv data/benchmark/latest.csv
python  scripts/benchmark.py    data/benchmark/latest.csv \
         --output                data/benchmark/latest.png
```

## Methodology

* **Hardware.** Colab T4 (sm_75, 16 GB, CUDA 12.8).
* **Dataset.** 10 synthetic H&E patches at 256x256 RGB, generated by
  `scripts/generate_sample_data.py`.
* **Pipeline parameters.** Default (`--num-streams 4`, `--pinned`,
  `--target default`).
* **CPU reference.** Single-threaded Macenko, no SIMD, compiled with
  `-O2`.
* **Metric.** Wall time per image. Each pipeline stage is timed with
  a `cudaEvent_t` on the same stream; CPU baseline is the wall time
  of the host-side function.

## Latest numbers (256x256 RGB)

```
stainkit: 0.1.0 — processing 10 image(s) from "data/raw"
stainkit: using GPU: Tesla T4
  [1/10] sample_000  GPU=6.65ms  CPU=6.83ms
  [2/10] sample_001  GPU=5.30ms  CPU=5.93ms
  [3/10] sample_002  GPU=5.07ms  CPU=5.95ms
  [4/10] sample_003  GPU=4.92ms  CPU=5.96ms
  [5/10] sample_004  GPU=4.92ms  CPU=5.78ms
  [6/10] sample_005  GPU=4.80ms  CPU=6.05ms
  [7/10] sample_006  GPU=4.97ms  CPU=5.59ms
  [8/10] sample_007  GPU=5.58ms  CPU=6.79ms
  [9/10] sample_008  GPU=4.93ms  CPU=5.82ms
  [10/10] sample_009 GPU=5.03ms  CPU=5.79ms
```

Average GPU time per image: **5.22 ms**.
Average CPU time per image: **6.05 ms**.
**Average speedup: 1.16x.**

## Stage breakdown

| Stage             | GPU (ms/img) | CPU (ms/img) | Speedup |
| ----------------- | -----------: | -----------: | ------: |
| H2D copy          |        0.26  |        0.00  |     —   |
| Color deconvolve  |        0.34  |        ~3.5  |    ~10x |
| Macenko normalise |        1.19  |        ~2.0  |    ~2x  |
| Tissue mask       |        1.40  |        ~1.2  |    ~1x  |
| D2H copy          |        2.04  |        0.00  |     —   |
| **Total**         |    **5.22**  |    **6.05**  |  **1.16x** |

CPU stage breakdown is estimated by summing the inner loop work; the
host baseline implementation is single-pass.

## Notes

* On 256x256 patches the GPU is barely faster than the CPU because
  launch overhead and host-device transfers dominate the actual kernel
  work. The morphology pass is the most expensive GPU stage (dilation
  + erosion is purely memory-bound and benefits from parallelism).
* The D2H copy is ~40% of total GPU time at this size because we copy
  both the normalised RGB image and the tissue mask back to the host.
  A real production deployment could leave both on the device if the
  caller does not need to write them to disk.
* For larger images (1024x1024 and above) the GPU speedup grows
  significantly because the kernel work is proportional to `npix`
  while the launch overhead is constant.
* The Otsu threshold stage is implemented both on the device and on
  the host. The CLI uses the device version by default; the host
  variant is useful for debugging.

## Reproducing

```bash
./install.sh
python scripts/generate_sample_data.py --num-images 10 --output data/raw
./build/bin/stainkit --input data/raw --output data/processed \
                     --benchmark --csv data/benchmark/latest.csv
python scripts/benchmark.py data/benchmark/latest.csv
```

The bundled `colab_notebook.ipynb` runs the benchmark end-to-end and
commits the CSV + PNG back to the repo.
